pack_name: Energy and Power Sector Compliance Overlay
description: Sector-specific screening controls for UK electricity, gas, supply, generation, distribution, transmission, smart-meter, customer-protection, and regulatory compliance documents.

metadata.scope: UK sector overlay controls for Energy and Power Sector Compliance Overlay. Intended to be used with uk_core_control_library.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from official UK legal/regulatory sources available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.
metadata.territorial_scope_note: UK law can differ across England, Wales, Scotland, and Northern Ireland. This starter pack uses UK-wide sources where available and flags devolved or nation-specific application for human review.

primary_sources.name: Apply for a gas or electricity licence
Energy consumer protection and complaints materials

rules.title: Ofgem gas/electricity licence evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for Ofgem licence, electricity generation/supply/distribution/transmission, gas supplier/transporter/shipper, or interconnector licence evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Standard licence condition compliance evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for standard licence conditions, special conditions, codes, compliance statements, or regulatory reporting evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Energy consumer-treatment and complaint-handling evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for complaints, redress, vulnerability, tariff disclosure, billing, switching, or customer-service evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
source_basis: Ofgem consumer standards and complaints materials

rules.title: Metering, smart-meter, or data-handling evidence is present where applicable
rules.summary: Checks for metering, smart meter, DCC, meter accuracy, consumption data, or data-access evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: No evidence of unlicensed energy-market activity is present
rules.summary: Screens for phrases suggesting supply, transport, distribution, or generation without required licensing.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
